title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence · Execution Trace: Case Design
summary: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa case design increment in an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
